# Time Machine Exclusions for Mobile App Development

A guide to excluding regeneratable caches, build artifacts, and SDK installations from Time Machine backups. Reduces backup size, speeds up backups, and minimizes wear on the NAS — without losing anything irreplaceable.

**Rule of thumb:** if it can be regenerated by re-running a build, re-downloading a package, or re-installing an SDK, exclude it.

---

## Why Exclude These

Mobile app development generates **massive amounts of churn**:

- Xcode `DerivedData` regenerates every build (gigabytes per project)
- Gradle caches re-download dependencies and recompile
- Flutter `pub-cache` is just a copy of packages from pub.dev
- Simulator and emulator images are huge and reinstallable
- iOS/Android SDKs can be re-downloaded from Apple/Google

Including these in Time Machine means:

- **Initial backup is 30–50% larger than necessary**
- **Hourly incremental backups are constantly large** (high churn = lots of new blocks every hour)
- **NAS storage fills faster** with redundant snapshots
- **Backup verification takes longer**

Excluding them costs you **nothing** — these directories are by definition recoverable.

---

## How to Add Exclusions

### GUI method (recommended for one-off folders)

1. **System Settings → General → Time Machine**
2. Click **Options...**
3. Click the **+** button under "Exclude from backups"
4. Navigate to the folder you want to exclude
   - **Tip:** Press **Cmd + Shift + .** in the file dialog to show hidden folders (anything starting with `.`)
5. Select the folder and click **Exclude**
6. Repeat for each path
7. Click **Done**

### Terminal method (faster for bulk exclusions)

```bash
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData
```

Use `-p` (path-based) so the exclusion follows the path even if folders are deleted and recreated. Without `-p`, the exclusion is by inode and breaks when the folder is regenerated.

To verify exclusions:

```bash
tmutil isexcluded ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData
```

To remove an exclusion:

```bash
sudo tmutil removeexclusion -p ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData
```

---

## iOS / Xcode / Swift

### Critical exclusions

```
~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData
~/Library/Developer/Xcode/Archives
~/Library/Developer/Xcode/iOS DeviceSupport
~/Library/Developer/Xcode/macOS DeviceSupport
~/Library/Developer/Xcode/watchOS DeviceSupport
~/Library/Developer/Xcode/tvOS DeviceSupport
~/Library/Developer/CoreSimulator
~/Library/Caches/com.apple.dt.Xcode
~/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm
```

### Notes

- **`DerivedData`** — Xcode's build output cache. Regenerated on every build. Easily 10–50 GB across active projects.
- **`Archives`** — only matters if you keep historical builds for App Store submissions. If you upload to App Store Connect, Apple keeps them. Safe to exclude.
- **`*DeviceSupport`** — symbol files for connected devices. Re-downloaded on first connect.
- **`CoreSimulator`** — entire simulator runtimes and device snapshots. **Often the biggest single offender** — can hit 50–100 GB. Always exclude.
- **SwiftPM cache** — package manager downloads, fully regeneratable.

### What NOT to exclude

- `~/Library/Developer/Xcode/UserData` — contains your code snippets, themes, and breakpoints. Small and irreplaceable. **Keep this backed up.**
- `~/Library/MobileDevice/Provisioning Profiles` — provisioning profiles. Annoying to regenerate. **Keep backed up.**

---

## Android / Kotlin / Java

### Critical exclusions

```
~/.gradle/caches
~/.gradle/daemon
~/.gradle/wrapper
~/Library/Android/sdk
~/Library/Android/sdk-tools
~/.android/avd
~/.android/cache
~/.android/build-cache
~/Library/Caches/Google/AndroidStudio*
~/Library/Logs/Google/AndroidStudio*
```

### Notes

- **`~/.gradle/caches`** — downloaded dependencies and compiled outputs. Regenerated automatically on next build.
- **`~/Library/Android/sdk`** — entire Android SDK including platform-tools, NDKs, system images. Can exceed 30 GB. Re-installable via Android Studio's SDK Manager.
- **`~/.android/avd`** — Android Virtual Device snapshots. Each AVD is several GB.
- **Android Studio caches** — IDE indexes, fully regeneratable.

### What NOT to exclude

- **Keystores** — your `*.jks` / `*.keystore` files for app signing. **Lose these and you cannot update your apps in the Play Store.** If they live anywhere under `~/.android/` or `~/Library/Android/`, **explicitly back them up** (better: store them in a password manager or encrypted vault, not just TM).
- `~/.gradle/init.d` — custom Gradle init scripts, if you have any.

---

## Flutter / Dart

### Critical exclusions

```
~/.pub-cache
~/fvm
~/.dartServer
~/Library/Caches/com.google.flutter
```

### Per-project exclusions (within each Flutter/Melos project)

These appear inside every Flutter project and should be excluded project-by-project, or by adding their parent dev folder and using a `.metadata_never_index` marker (see below):

```
build/
.dart_tool/
.flutter-plugins
.flutter-plugins-dependencies
ios/Pods/
ios/.symlinks/
android/.gradle/
android/build/
```

### Notes

- **`~/.pub-cache`** — pub.dev package cache. Regenerated by `flutter pub get` / `dart pub get`. Often 5–15 GB.
- **`~/fvm`** — Flutter Version Management installs. Each Flutter SDK version is 1–3 GB. Reinstallable via `fvm install <version>`.
- **`build/`** and **`.dart_tool/`** in projects — Flutter/Dart build outputs. Regenerated on every build.
- **`ios/Pods/`** — CocoaPods install. Regenerated by `pod install`.

### Bulk-exclude technique for Melos monorepos

For your `icodeforyou_flutter` monorepo with many packages, manually adding `build/` and `.dart_tool/` for each package is tedious. Instead, drop a marker file in each package directory:

```bash
# From the monorepo root, mark all build dirs
find . -type d -name "build" -exec touch {}/.metadata_never_index \;
find . -type d -name ".dart_tool" -exec touch {}/.metadata_never_index \;
```

The `.metadata_never_index` file tells **Spotlight** not to index the folder — which is also a strong hint to TM that the folder is transient. However, it's **not a guaranteed TM exclusion** — for that, use `tmutil addexclusion` per path or add the parent dev folder.

**More reliable approach for monorepos:** add a `.nobackup` directory at a known location and symlink build outputs there, or simply exclude the entire monorepo's `build/` and `.dart_tool/` parent — accepting that you'll re-add it if you reorganize.

### What NOT to exclude

- **`pubspec.yaml`** and **`pubspec.lock`** — your dependency manifest. **Keep backed up.**
- **`melos.yaml`** — monorepo config. **Keep backed up.**
- Source code — obviously. Git handles versioning, but TM is your safety net for uncommitted changes.

---

## Node / npm / Web (if you do any web work alongside)

### Critical exclusions

```
~/.npm
~/.yarn
~/.pnpm-store
~/Library/Caches/Yarn
```

### Per-project

```
node_modules/
.next/
dist/
build/
.cache/
```

### Notes

- **`node_modules/`** is the textbook example of "regeneratable churn." Always exclude.

---

## Other Mac Developer Caches

### Worth excluding

```
~/Library/Caches
~/Library/Logs
~/Library/Application Support/CrashReporter
~/.cache
~/.cocoapods/repos
~/Library/Caches/CocoaPods
~/Library/Caches/Homebrew
```

### Notes

- **`~/Library/Caches`** is broad — contains caches from every app. Some apps regenerate slowly, but the space savings are significant. Apple-recommended exclusion.
- **Homebrew cache** — downloaded `.tar` archives of installed formulae. `brew cleanup` clears them anyway.
- **CocoaPods repos** — the local CocoaPods specs cache. Regenerated by `pod setup`.

### What NOT to exclude

- **`~/Library/Application Support`** (the parent) — contains app data for many apps you do want backed up. Exclude specific subfolders only if needed, not the whole parent.
- **`~/Library/Keychains`** — your keychain. Critical. Never exclude.

---

## Things to KEEP Backed Up (Don't Accidentally Exclude)

- **`~/Documents`**, **`~/Desktop`**, **`~/Downloads`** (if you use it for active work)
- **`~/Library/Keychains`** — passwords, certs, dev signing keys
- **`~/Library/Mobile Documents`** — iCloud Drive (small but contains sync metadata)
- **`~/Library/Mail`** — local mail data
- **`~/.ssh`** — SSH keys. Critical.
- **`~/.gnupg`** — GPG keys, if you use them.
- **`~/.config`** — dotfile configs for various tools
- **`~/.zshrc`**, **`~/.bashrc`**, **`~/.profile`**, etc.
- **App signing certificates and provisioning profiles** (iOS)
- **Android keystores** (Play Store signing)
- **Source code repositories** — Git is great, but TM is a safety net for uncommitted work

---

## Verification After Setup

### Check what's excluded

```bash
sudo mdfind "com_apple_backup_excludeItem == 'com.apple.backupd'"
```

### Check estimated backup size

After applying exclusions, run a Time Machine backup and check:

**System Settings → General → Time Machine → Options → ⓘ icon next to a destination**

This shows the size of the next backup. Compare before and after exclusions — you should see **30–50% reduction** for an active mobile dev machine.

### Spot-check exclusion is working

```bash
tmutil compare -X | head -50
```

Shows what would be backed up next. Confirm none of your excluded paths appear.

---

## Maintenance

- **Recheck exclusions every 6 months** — new tools and SDKs add new cache locations
- **After upgrading Xcode or Android Studio** — verify cache paths haven't moved
- **When adding new dev tools** — check their docs for cache locations and add them
- **When TM backups suddenly grow** — something new started churning. Check `tmutil compare` to identify the culprit

---

## Quick Reference: Paste-Ready Terminal Commands

Run these to add all the major exclusions at once:

```bash
# iOS / Xcode / Swift
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/Archives
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Developer/CoreSimulator
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Caches/com.apple.dt.Xcode
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm

# Android / Kotlin
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/.gradle/caches
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/.gradle/daemon
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Android/sdk
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/.android/avd

# Flutter / Dart
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/.pub-cache
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/fvm
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/.dartServer

# General Mac
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Caches
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/Library/Logs
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/.npm
sudo tmutil addexclusion -p ~/.yarn
```

Verify each:

```bash
tmutil isexcluded <path>
```

---

**Last note:** exclusions take effect on the **next** backup, not retroactively. After applying, your *next* TM backup will be smaller; existing snapshots already on the NAS still contain the now-excluded data until they age out of rotation.
